name: kubeleash
description: Kubernetes MCP server with RBAC-style, context-scoped guardrails for AI agents.
long_description: kubeleash is a Kubernetes MCP server with a twist: RBAC-style, context-scoped access control for AI agents. Point it at a kubeconfig — even a cluster-admin one — and a local policy file constrains what the agent can actually do, per kube context. Destructive actions are gated before any call reaches the cluster. Deny wins, default-deny, and a broken policy refuses to start — it never fails open. Runs entirely locally over stdio; there is no remote/hosted connector and zero telemetry.

policy_path.title: Policy file
policy_path.description: Path to your kubeleash policy.yaml. Required — kubeleash is default-deny and refuses to start without an explicit policy.

kubeconfig_path.title: Kubeconfig file
kubeconfig_path.description: Path to the kubeconfig kubeleash should use. Defaults to ~/.kube/config.

log_level.title: Log level
log_level.description: Audit/diagnostics log level: debug, info, warn, or error. The audit log is JSON on stderr.

tools.name: k8s_list
tools.description: List Kubernetes objects of a resource type, optionally filtered by namespace and selectors.

tools.name: k8s_get
tools.description: Get a single Kubernetes object by name.

tools.name: k8s_apply
tools.description: Server-side apply a Kubernetes manifest (create or update depending on existence).

tools.name: k8s_delete
tools.description: Delete a single Kubernetes object by name.

tools.name: k8s_exec
tools.description: Execute a command in a pod container.

tools.name: k8s_scale
tools.description: Scale a workload to a desired replica count.

tools.name: k8s_capabilities
tools.description: Report which actions the policy allows in a given kube context. Does not contact the cluster.
